title: Car carrying family of 3 pulled from Seekonk River in Rhode Island
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A car with a family of three went into the Seekonk River, Rhode Island, Sunday evening near Taft Street.
Bystanders on a jet ski attempted rescue, and 911 was called; first responders arrived within three minutes.
Authorities have not confirmed the identities or conditions of the occupants as recovery efforts continue.

title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes its Downtown Boston restaurant, ending its brick-and-mortar presence after nearly seven years.
The business will focus exclusively on catering and its food truck, with scheduled stops in Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common.
Chef Ernie Campbell started Jamaica Mi Hungry in 2012, earning accolades like Best Food Truck and Best Jamaican Restaurant.

title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A report found former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used offensive language targeting ethnic groups, minorities, and protected classes.
Goode's racist, misogynistic, and other derogatory messages led to his resignation and suspension of his police certification.
Canton placed Goode on leave last October, learning of the messages amid an internal probe linked to Karen Read's lawsuit.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr. was sentenced to life in prison for raping five women and attempting to assault a sixth.
Campbell posed as an Uber driver to attack women outside Boston bars between 2017 and 2019.
Campbell, brother of Attorney General Andrea Campbell, was found guilty of 21 out of 22 charges.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, from Haverhill, died in a wrong-way crash on I-93 in Boston Sunday morning.
81-year-old Antone Carvalho drove the wrong way on I-93, causing a multi-vehicle crash near Exit 15A.
The family of four involved in the collision was hospitalized; police are investigating, with charges pending.

title: 5 takeaways from Paraguay’s upset win over Germany in World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany 4-3 in penalty kicks at Boston Stadium to reach the World Cup Round of 16.
A video review overturned Germany's extra-time goal due to a foul, preserving Paraguay's chance in the match.
Julio Enciso scored Paraguay's first knockout-round goal, while Jose Canale netted the decisive penalty kick.

title: Revolution announce front office shift: Mass. native Chris Tierney named general manager
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney named New England Revolution's general manager and chief soccer officer, promoted from assistant sporting director.
Curt Onalfo shifts to senior vice president of soccer operations, focusing on team development with the Pro Pathway and academy.
Both Tierney and Onalfo will report to club president Brian Bilello in their new roles at the Revolution.

title: Man allegedly pepper sprays woman jogging in apparent random assault in Maine coastal city
author: John Waller
bullets: A 37-year-old man was arrested for allegedly pepper spraying a woman jogging in Rockland, Maine.
The suspect faces charges including criminal threatening, terrorizing, and criminal use of disabling chemicals.
Witnesses reportedly chased off the attacker, and police consider the act a random assault.

title: Noah Kahan asks his audience to please not poop at venues (except in designated areas)
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Noah Kahan urges concertgoers to use designated restrooms after an incident at his Philadelphia concert.
Kahan asked fans to stop stealing the Alger Brook Road sign in his hometown of Strafford.
Olivia Rodrigo noted some fans wear diapers to concerts to avoid leaving their spots for restrooms.

title: Hiker rescued by helicopter after 50-foot fall in Huntington Ravine
author: John Waller
bullets: A Quebec City man, 24, was rescued after a 50-foot fall on Mount Washington's Huntington Ravine Trail.
Rescue crews and a National Guard helicopter responded to the scene around 12:30 p.m. for the steep terrain rescue.
The hiker suffered serious injuries and was flown to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center; the trail is known for dangers.
